storyboard.content: I'm having really bad shortness of breath that keeps getting worse. I can barely do anything without feeling like I can't catch my breath.

I've been coughing up thick yellow mucus. It's not clear — it's definitely yellow and there's a lot of it. I've started wheezing on top of everything else. It's a new thing that started as things got worse.
My chest feels really tight. It's this constant pressure that doesn't go away.
The breathlessness is especially bad when I lie down at night. I've been struggling to sleep because of it.
I've used my rescue inhaler six times in the last 24 hours and it's barely helping anymore. It used to give me relief but now it's not doing much.
This has been getting progressively worse over the past week. It started mild but has steadily gone downhill.
I can barely climb one flight of stairs without having to stop. Basic tasks are becoming really difficult to manage.
I'm a 42-year-old woman with a history of mild asthma. I have a rescue inhaler that I use when I need it.
